The Collective

Questioning the Mechanics of Art

Leroy Brothers have spent over two decades interrogating the systems that construct artistic value and identity in the contemporary art world.

Timeline

1997 — Present

1997

Formation

Leroy Brothers collective founded in Belgium

2003

MySpace Era

Pioneering social media portraiture

2006

Artmann Gallery

Outsourced painters, prêt-à-crocher collections

2010

Axel King

Cult of the superstar — art created with crowdsourced creativity

2013

Witness Your World

Crowdsourced art creation as collective awareness

2016

Witness My Face

Interactive installation at Pukkelpop — short-lived celebrity as participation

2021

NFT Collection

Entering blockchain art space

2023

Arora Vale

AI artist collaboration begins

2025

Present

Mechanisms of art in an AI-driven society

Scroll to explore
Biography

Working at the intersection of conceptual art, digital culture, and institutional critique, the Leroy Brothers have spent over two decades interrogating the systems that construct artistic value and identity in the contemporary art world.

Their practice spans painting, installation, performance, and digital media, often creating elaborate fictions—fictional artists, fictional brands, fictional worlds—that reveal the underlying mechanisms of the art market and celebrity culture.

Notable projects include the MySpace Portrait Paintings (2003-2006), which documented the birth of social media self-presentation; Witness Your World (2013-2015), a series of interactive installations transforming social media into collaborative artworks; and Axel King (2010), a fictional artist persona that operated within real art world contexts.

Philosophy
"We are interested in the space between authenticity and construction, where identity becomes performance and commerce becomes critique."
— Leroy Brothers
